query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Pseudapostle', 'One falsely claiming to be an apostle', 'Critics argued that the plan might pseudapostle the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pseudonym', 'A fictitious name, often used by writers and movie stars', 'The essay highlights the pseudonym of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pseudonymity', 'Use of a false name', 'The essay highlights the pseudonymity of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Psychiatry', 'The science of diagnosing and treating mental disorders', 'The essay highlights the psychiatry of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Psychopathic', 'Having the behaviors and personality traits of a psychopath', 'The reviewer described the tone as psychopathic, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Psychotherapy', 'The treatment of psychological disorders', 'The essay highlights the psychotherapy of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pudgy', 'Overweight (pertaining particularly to children), plump', 'Over time, the community learned to pudgy its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Puerile', 'Childishly silly and trivial', 'What seemed puerile at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pugnacious', 'Eager or quick to argue or fight', 'The reviewer described the tone as pugnacious, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Puissant', 'Powerful, mighty, having authority', 'Scholars noted a striking puissant between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pulchritude', 'Physical beauty', 'The essay highlights the pulchritude of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pulmonary', 'Pertaining to, having, or affecting the lungs', 'Her pulmonary became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Punctilio', 'A fine point or detail regarding conduct or procedure', 'The essay highlights the punctilio of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Punctilious', 'Paying attention to detail', 'The author hoped the new policy would punctilious tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pundit', 'A learned person, expert or authority', 'Her pundit became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pungent', 'Sharply affecting the organs of taste or smell', 'The passage presents a pungent portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Punitive', 'Concerned with or inflicting punishment', 'The passage presents a punitive portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pupilage', 'The condition of being a pupil', 'The essay highlights the pupilage of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Purgatory', 'In catholicism, a place where souls are purified after death', 'Scholars noted a striking purgatory between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Purist', 'A person who insists on purity in language, style, etc', 'The essay highlights the purist of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Purl', 'To finish with loops or a looped edging', 'Over time, the community learned to purl its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Purloin', 'To take dishonestly', 'The author hoped the new policy would purloin tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Purport', 'To convey, imply, or profess outwardly, often falsely', 'Critics argued that the plan might purport the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Purveyor', 'Someone who supplies what is needed, especially food', 'Her purveyor became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Purview', 'The range of operation, authority, control, etc', 'Her purview became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pusillanimous', 'Lacking courage', 'What seemed pusillanimous at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Putative', 'Commonly believed or deemed to be the case', 'Over time, the community learned to putative its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Putrescent', 'Becoming putrid, putrefying', 'The reviewer described the tone as putrescent, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Putrid', 'Rotting, rotten, being in a state of putrefaction', 'The essay highlights the putrid of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pyre', 'A heap of wood or other combustible material', 'The essay highlights the pyre of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pyromania', 'An uncontrollable urge to start fires', 'The author hoped the new policy would pyromania tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pyrotechnic', 'Of, or relating to fireworks', 'The author hoped the new policy would pyrotechnic tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Pyx', 'The small container used to hold the eucharist', 'Over time, the community learned to pyx its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Quack', 'To make a noise like a duck', 'Critics argued that the plan might quack the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Quackery', 'The practice of fraudulent medicine', 'Scholars noted a striking quackery between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Quadrate', 'Square or rectangular', 'The author hoped the new policy would quadrate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Quadruple', 'To multiply something by four times', 'Over time, the community learned to quadruple its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Quaff', 'To drink something with hearty enjoyment, especially alcohol', 'The author hoped the new policy would quaff tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Quagmire', 'A complex or hazardous situation', 'Her quagmire became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Quail', 'To lose heart or courage in difficulty or danger', 'The author hoped the new policy would quail tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Quaint', 'Old-fashioned', 'Scholars noted a striking quaint between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Qualify', 'To describe something by listing its qualities', 'Over time, the community learned to qualify its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Qualitative', 'Relating to the characteristics or qualities of something', '“The research focused on qualitative data rather than numerical statistics.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Qualm', 'A sickly feeling of being ill at ease, sudden queasiness', 'The essay highlights the qualm of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Quandary', 'A state of uncertainty or confusion', '“She was in a quandary about which college to choose.”', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Quantifiable', 'Able to be measured or counted', '“The economic benefits of the project are easily quantifiable.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Quantify', 'Measure and express as a number', 'The author hoped the new policy would quantify tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Quarantine', 'Isolation imposed to prevent the spread of disease', 'Over time, the community learned to quarantine its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Quarrelsome', 'Argumentative', 'The essay highlights the quarrelsome of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Quarry', 'A pit from which stone, slate, etc  is obtained', 'Her quarry became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
